title: HVR Energy and Q8 Spain Sign MoU for Hydrogen Refueling Network in Spain
content: HVR Energy and Q8 Energy Red have signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) to collaborate on the deployment of the INICIA hydrogen refueling network at existing service stations in Spain. The agreement, signed between the companies, establishes a framework for cooperation to accelerate the availability of renewable hydrogen in strategic corridors across the country.INICIA is HVR Energyâ€™s hydrogen refueling model designed for rapid deployment, low installed capacity, and a reduced footprint, with dispensing at 350 bar, facilitating its operational integration into existing assets and the safe and efficient start-up of demand. The logistical approachâ€”receiving compressed hydrogen (â‰¥500 bar or higher)â€”minimizes on-site compression and storage, optimizing CAPEX and OPEX and contributing to Spainâ€™s objectives.The MoU establishes a first phase with the deployment of up to two stations by March 2026, with Badajoz and CÃ³rdoba as prioritized sites. HVR Energy will be responsible for installation, commissioning, and maintenance, as well as hydrogen supply; Q8 will be responsible for sales to the end customer.
author: Analysts Account
